what_it_does: It defines the completion criteria (a truth-table) for building the operator surface: a grouped checklist where every item must pass both a FUNCTION bar (real state moves verified by use) and a FORM bar (design-system-correct product face verified by a separate design-critic agent), plus a verification protocol, status markers, preserve-checks, drift gate, and dependency-first priority order.
observations: Every criterion is two-faced: FUNCTION (real state moves, no stubs) and FORM (design-system tokens/components, verified by a design-critic agent, not the implementer).
Verification is by actual use of the bridge-served surface, not by reading code, file existence, or DOM queries.
A DESIGN-LINT machine gate fails builds that use hardcoded values or bespoke elements outside the design system.
A DRIFT GATE requires company suites, coherence/orienteering detectors, and concurrency acceptance tests to stay green.
Each item includes a preserve-check naming what must keep working (e.g., other /api consumers, canvas app, MCP face, cc_channels live injection).
Status markers are explicit: ☐ unchecked, ✅ verified-by-use, ⚠ designed-not-verified, ✗ broken/debt.

Groups are dependency-ordered: F foundation, B fused shell, H home, I inbox, C chat/RHM, R run-monitor, S search, M comments, V versions, A attribution, RT routing/spawn, P projects/self, AR artefacts, PF product face.
Some items are explicitly waiting (F2 waits for B-shell injection migration; I2 depends on bless/proposal seams; V1 depends on F3; AR2 is coupled with S8+S9+S10).
Human gates are marked for Tim: aesthetic taste-shaping, the rule-engine-dispatch constitutional edit (RT2), and RHM two-modes confirmation.
B4 operator-token auth must ship before any tailnet exposure.
standouts: The two-faced FUNCTION/FORM verification-by-use rule is the central quality gate.
Design evaluation is explicitly outsourced to a separate browser-driving, screenshot-taking design-critic agent.
Preserve-checks and drift gate make backwards-compatibility and system health first-class completion requirements.
Priority order is dependency-first and explicitly gates tailnet exposure behind auth and human approval behind Tim.
conventions: Uses grouped checklists with symbolic status markers and a consistent FUNCTION/FORM/preserves structure per item.
Names dependencies and ordering constraints explicitly (e.g., F2 waits on B-shell, B4 before tailnet).
Mandates machine gates (design-lint, drift gate) alongside human gates (Tim flags, design-critic).
Breaks the common pattern of accepting code-read or file-existence as done by requiring operational verification.
Uses human-register language for empty/loading/error states and avoids engine labels in user-facing surfaces.
concerns: The file references many external components (runtime/bridge.py, session_channels, cc_channels, tokens.json, design system) without defining them here, so the criteria are not self-contained.
Several criteria depend on seams that are not yet built (F2, I2, V1, AR2), making the checklist partly speculative.
The design-critic agent and acceptance-suite processes are invoked but their concrete rubrics and invocation paths are not specified.
Subjective taste calls are flagged for Tim, but the boundary between objective design-system compliance and subjective taste is not fully operationalized.
The density of cross-cutting requirements (preserve-checks, drift gate, auth, concurrency) may make per-item green/red status hard to determine in practice.
notes: A two-faced, use-verified checklist that forces both working state moves and design-system-correct surfaces while protecting existing consumers and routing taste/constitutional decisions to Tim.
questions: What is the exact schema and lifecycle of session_channels and the cc_channels live-injection mechanism?
How is the design-critic agent invoked, and where is its detailed rubric stored?
What are the file paths and contents of the acceptance suites for each company-improvement group?
What is the implementation of the classify(w,h) responsive switch and the tokens.json/emit.py axis system?
What does the rule-engine-dispatch constitutional edit in RT2 entail?
What are S8, S9, and S10, and how do they couple with AR2?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file presents itself as the truth-table the loop runs and delivers exactly that—a structured set of completion criteria, verification protocol, and priority order.
apparent_themes: Verification by real use, not inspection.
Dual quality: function must move state, form must be design-system-correct.
Preserve existing behavior and system health while adding new surfaces.
Fail-loud machine gates plus human gates for subjective/constitutional decisions.
Operator-centric, navigable, visual surfaces rather than text walls.
intention_signals: It is trying to stop an AI build loop from declaring work done based on code appearance alone, by forcing every item to prove itself through live use and independent design critique while keeping the rest of the system green.
novelty: The explicit two-faced FUNCTION/FORM bar, the mandatory preserve-check per item, the separation of implementer and design-critic, and the dependency-first priority order with human gates make this more than a typical todo list.
contribution: It contributes a rigorous, verifiable completion framework: the two-faced FUNCTION/FORM criteria, the verification-by-use protocol, the preserve-check and drift-gate invariants, the dependency-first priority order, and the explicit human/machine gate semantics.
summary_for_embedding: A completion-criteria document for the operator-surface build that defines a two-faced FUNCTION/FORM verification protocol, grouped checklists with preserve-checks and status markers, machine and human gates, and a dependency-first priority order.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the build loop with an objective truth-table for declaring operator-surface items complete only after live use and independent design critique, while protecting existing consumers and routing sensitive decisions to Tim.
suggested_fields: verification_evidence: the artifact or test run that proves a criterion is green.
depends_on: explicit upstream criterion or component dependencies.
human_gate_owner: the person who must approve a subjective or constitutional item.
preserve_targets: the list of consumers/systems a criterion must not break.
acceptance_suite_path: where the acceptance suite for a foundation item lives.
proposed_edge_kinds: verifies: a criterion is verified by use of a specific surface or API.
preserves: an item must keep another consumer or surface working.
blocks: one criterion gates or must precede another.
waits_on_human: an item requires human approval before proceeding.
design-critiques: the design-critic agent evaluates a surface against the form bar.
